title: Social sites mangle scoped npm commands: use unscoped alias kage-graph-mcp in all posts
summary: X and Reddit auto convert @kage core in 'npx y @kage core/kage graph mcp' into a user mention u/kage core on Reddit , breaking the command. Permanent fix shipped 2026 06 13: published unscoped alias package 'kage graph m
body: X and Reddit auto-convert @kage-core in 'npx -y @kage-core/kage-graph-mcp' into a user mention (u/kage-core on Reddit), breaking the command. Permanent fix shipped 2026-06-13: published unscoped alias package 'kage-graph-mcp' (alias/kage-graph-mcp in repo) that depends on @kage-core/kage-graph-mcp ^2.2.3 and forwards bins via 2-line shims. Verified clean-room: npm install kage-graph-mcp -> dep 2.2.3, bin works. ALWAYS use 'npx -y kage-graph-mcp ...' in social copy; scoped stays canonical in README/site/registry. Gotcha hit while debugging: npx prefers an existing PATH binary over the registry — a stale global install (was 2.0.0 here) silently serves old behavior; fix with npm install -g @kage-core/kage-graph-mcp@latest. Keep alias version bumped at each release (publish from alias/kage-graph-mcp).
